name: Visenya Targaryen
disambiguator: eldest of the three siblings; sister-wife of Aegon; wields Dark Sister; rides Vhagar

name: Rhaenys Targaryen
disambiguator: youngest of the three siblings; sister-wife of Aegon; rides Meraxes

name: Gaemon Targaryen
disambiguator: "Gaemon the Glorious"; brother & husband to Daenys; Lord of Dragonstone after Aenar

name: Elaena Targaryen
disambiguator: daughter of Gaemon the Glorious; ruled Dragonstone with brother Aegon

name: Aerys Targaryen
disambiguator: brother of Maegon; son of Aegon (son of Gaemon); father of Aelyx, Baelon, Daemion

name: Baelon Targaryen
disambiguator: son of Aerys; brother of Aelyx and Daemion

name: Aerion Targaryen
disambiguator: son of Daemion; Lord of Dragonstone; father of Aegon the Conqueror

name: Daemon Velaryon
disambiguator: Lord of the Tides; made master of ships; Aegon's admiral; slain off Gulltown

name: Torrhen Stark
disambiguator: King in the North of Winterfell; "the King Who Knelt"; made Warden of the North

name: Ronnel Arryn
disambiguator: boy king of the Eyrie; son of Sharra; made a lord under Aegon

name: Manfred Hightower
disambiguator: Lord of Oldtown; cautious and godly; submitted to Aegon

name: High Septon
disambiguator: Father of the Faithful at the Starry Sept; anointed Aegon; had the Crone's vision

name: Balerion
disambiguator: "the Black Dread"; Aegon's mount; sole survivor of Aenar's five dragons

name: Dark Sister
disambiguator: Valyrian longsword carried by Visenya

name: Blackfyre
disambiguator: Valyrian steel blade borne by Aegon

name: Painted Table
disambiguator: slab carved in the shape of Westeros at Lord Aegon's command

name: Iron Throne
disambiguator: metal seat forged from the melted blades of Aegon's fallen foes

name: House Targaryen
disambiguator: dragonlords of Dragonstone, of Valyrian blood

name: House Velaryon
disambiguator: of Driftmark; Valyrian descent; sworn to House Targaryen

name: House Celtigar
disambiguator: of Claw Isle; allied Valyrian house; sworn to House Targaryen

name: House Durrandon
disambiguator: Storm Kings of Storm's End; ended with Argilac

name: House Hoare
disambiguator: ironborn Kings of the Isles and the Rivers; ended with Harren

name: House Stark
disambiguator: Kings in the North of Winterfell

name: House Martell
disambiguator: Martell princes of Dorne; seat Sunspear

name: House Lannister
disambiguator: Kings of the Rock at Casterly Rock

name: House Gardener
disambiguator: Kings of the Reach at Highgarden; ended at the Field of Fire

name: House Arryn
disambiguator: rulers of the Vale, the Fingers, and the Mountains of the Moon

name: House Baratheon
disambiguator: founded by Orys; took the crowned stag and words of the Durrandon

name: House Tully
disambiguator: of Riverrun; Edmyn Tully made Lord Paramount of the Trident

name: House Tyrell
disambiguator: stewards of Highgarden; raised to Warden of the South

name: House Hightower
disambiguator: of the Hightower in Oldtown; bannermen to the Gardeners

name: House Rowan
disambiguator: of Goldengrove

name: House Allyrion
disambiguator: of Godsgrace

name: House Redwyne
disambiguator: of the Arbor

name: House Darklyn
disambiguator: Darklyn kings of Duskendale

name: House Sunderland
disambiguator: of the Three Sisters; Marla proclaimed queen

name: House Morrigen
disambiguator: Dickon Morrigen

name: House Oakheart
disambiguator: Lord Oakheart at the Field of Fire

name: Freys
disambiguator: riverlords who rose against Harren

name: Faith Militant
disambiguator: fighting orders (Warrior's Sons); "the Stars and Swords"

name: the Faith
disambiguator: the Seven; centered at Oldtown under the High Septon

name: Dragonstone
disambiguator: island citadel; seat of House Targaryen

name: Valyria
disambiguator: the Freehold; destroyed by the Doom

name: Driftmark
disambiguator: seat of the Velaryons

name: Claw Isle
disambiguator: seat of the Celtigars

name: King's Landing
disambiguator: new town founded on the three hills at the Blackwater mouth

name: Oldtown
disambiguator: greatest city in Westeros; seat of the Hightowers; center of the Faith

name: Storm's End
disambiguator: seat of the Durrandons

name: Harrenhal
disambiguator: Harren's gigantic castle beside the Gods Eye; burned by Balerion

disambiguator: seat of the Lannisters

name: Highgarden
disambiguator: seat of the Gardeners, then the Tyrells

name: Winterfell
disambiguator: seat of the Starks

name: the Eyrie

name: Sunspear

disambiguator: river at whose mouth Aegon landed

disambiguator: river of the Reach

disambiguator: seat of the Mootons

name: Gulltown

name: the Three Sisters
disambiguator: site of the Sistermen's rebellion

name: Riverrun

name: Bronzegate

disambiguator: seat of House Rowan; the Two Kings joined there

disambiguator: town where both queens rejoined Aegon

disambiguator: Visenya took fealty there before the Field of Fire

disambiguator: northern stronghold some urged Torrhen fall back to

disambiguator: Torrhen crossed it into the riverlands

disambiguator: gateway through the Red Mountains, guarded by Dornishmen

disambiguator: Dornish castle found empty by Rhaenys

disambiguator: seat of House Allyrion; found deserted

disambiguator: Aegon may have visited; accounts differ

disambiguator: seat of the maesters at Oldtown

disambiguator: gateway to the Vale of Arryn

disambiguator: western sea Rhaenys wished to cross

disambiguator: home of the pirates who raided Cape Wrath

disambiguator: its "tigers" opposed by the alliance Argilac joined

disambiguator: site of the battle where Argilac slew Garse VII

disambiguator: future road near the Field of Fire
